title: One Battle After Another Wins Best Picture Comedy at 2026 Golden Globes
text: Paul Thomas Anderson's film "One Battle After Another" claimed the Golden Globe for Best Picture, Comedy or Musical, strengthening its position as a leading Oscar contender. The film also secured awards for Best Screenplay and Best Director for Anderson, plus Best Supporting Actress for Teyana Taylor. The victory follows the film's win at the Critics Choice Awards just one week prior. Reviewers praised the work as a paranoid comedy-thriller that captures contemporary anxieties. As IndieWire critic David Ehrlich noted, the film "accurately crystallize[s] how fucking anxious it feels to be alive right now." Anderson's film represents a significant gamble for Warner Bros., which backed the nine-figure budget—the director's most expensive project to date. Despite initial concerns about the studio executives' job security, the film succeeded commercially and critically, leading to contract extensions for studio leaders Pamela Abdy and Michael de Luca. The Comedy or Musical category at the Golden Globes traditionally encompasses films outside conventional genre definitions. This year's competitors included Richard Linklater's "Blue Moon," Yorgos Lanthimos' "Bugonia," Josh Safdie's "Marty Supreme," and Park Chan-wook's "No Other Choice."
